summary_one_line: Input must carry the FOURCC 'MCH5' (4d 43 48 35) as the colorspace signature so the switch hits case cmsSigMCH5Data.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace-signature field (offsets 0x10-0x13) must equal the literal 'MCH5' (4d 43 48 35), which decodes to cmsSigMCH5Data so the switch in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace selects the true (blocked) arm at line 905.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed carries bytes 4d 43 48 35 ('MCH5') at offsets 0x10-0x13 exactly where the loser seeds carry a different signature; this is the only constant the switch needs to land on case cmsSigMCH5Data and increment the branch hitcount to 1.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at offsets 0x10-0x13, decoding to cmsSigRgbData (a different switch arm at line 893), so they never reach case cmsSigMCH5Data and stay blocked across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer/string CMP for the colorspace signature and writes a matching-prefix/Hamming distance bucket into CMP_MAP; a mutation that gets the signature byte-closer to 'MCH5' than any seen pair adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, giving a gradient that climbs to the exact 4-byte FOURCC. naive lacks CMP interception, so it must blind-guess all 4 bytes via havoc and never lands them.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the switch arm case cmsSigMCH5Data at cmspcs.c:905, whose true side is globally blocked.
The blocking branch sits inside a switch on the colorspace signature in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace.
The value_profile winner-resolving seed carries the literal 'MCH5' at offsets 0x10-0x13.
The winner seed was discovered by the value_profile fuzzer.
The naive loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13 instead of 'MCH5'.
Offsets 0x10-0x13 differ between W and L with W concentrated on the MCH5 bytes and L on RGB bytes.
In the loser, the switch resolved to the RGB arm (case cmsSigRgbData taken 52 times) and never reached the MCH5 arm.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 893  T=0 F=1  T=52 F=14  case cmsSigRgbData:

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's mechanism is CMP interception writing a closeness bucket into CMP_MAP, retaining inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The distance bucket encodes matching-prefix length for string/memory comparisons, giving a byte-wise gradient toward the literal.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage I2S-floor check does not apply. The MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms value_profile uses no I2SRandReplace and that absence of dash rows is direct evidence the winning seed was found by value_profile rather than an I2S stage; the divergence is attributable to the value_profile technique (the one-technique delta between value_profile and naive).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a 4-byte switch on an input-resident FOURCC where value_profile fails to resolve the case arm faster/more often than naive, or where naive resolves the exact MCH5 literal as readily as value_profile.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner seed is shown, so the claim that value_profile climbed the prefix gradient (rather than getting lucky on the 4 bytes once) rests on the technique model plus the 10/10-vs-0/10 trial separation rather than on observed intermediate near-match corpus members.
